activities.description: With Tam and Herve. One large log, maybe 18” in diameter across the single track on the east side that needs a chain saw, and another 9”-er that we couldn’t budge. We cleared a few 6” logs and lots of brush.

activities.name: The Control of Nature
activities.description: In the ‘80s John McPhee wrote a book with this ironic title which featured a long essay about these mountains — the San Gabriels that rim the LA basin to the north. There is an ongoing battle between the San Andreas fault which is trying to raise the mountains and the storms like the ones we have had recently which are trying to wash the mountains down. The San Andreas is winning and the mountains are still ascending. Seeing one of the “debris dams” on this hike reminded me of the book, and the effort required to keep this trail passable on the ever-steepening slopes was a sober reminder of how puny our efforts sometimes are.

Just 8s behind my PR from 3 years ago. Maybe I can beat it.

Garmin’s “race an activity” feature is fun for this kind of stuff as it provides continuous ahead/behind feedback

activities.name: New shoes!

activities.description: Except when you keep getting faster on it. Done this 37 times in the last three years and today was the fastest by 20s. The secret was to warm up a little first :-)

activities.name: Trying to up the cadence
activities.description: 165 is natural and easy for me, but 170 is super tough somehow. I am either a finely tuned machine, or a rusty old bucket.

Backyard course; backyard pace
activities.description:
With Lauren! It was her idea, and a good one. 

Note the changed course for this year. Start on the outside track, pass the start/finish on the inside, then do another 1/2 mile north on that inside track. Climb the grassy slope up to the outside and then run back along the outside.

activities.name: Murakami miles in the morning
activities.description: When Haruki Murakami wrote “What I talk about when I talk about Running” (an excellent memoir) his practice was to run 6 miles in exactly an hour 6 days/week. I was a few seconds fast today… will try to nail it next time.

He intended to eat one of the salads, but when he saw how fast I ate mine, he gave me the other as well. Now *that’s* a good friend. Today we turned around at the so-called “Salad Garden”

We should come here together for 70m vertical repeats.

activities.description: Every year I run better in winter than in summer. This morning was cooler but not yet crisp. Hoping to take a minute off this course in the fall and maybe another in winter, like I did last year. Running-gods willing!

activities.name: Snake2lake scouting #2
activities.description: Attempting to find a place for an aid station between Rattlesnake and Tiger. Expected location was a big road-works site. With Monica. NO GO! Couldn’t get to the trail for all the blackberry

activities.name: Snake2lake scouting #3: Aid station, creek crossing, road works crossing
activities.description: 1 mile from a parking lot is where the aid station will be (where this run turned); then down to the creek (slow but quite easy); wet foot creek crossing ( not too cold); the we got escorted across the road works by Kevin, the very helpful environmental engineer on the site. All good.

activities.name: In praise of short runs
activities.description: When you finish wanting more, you erroneously think you could run forever. It's a good feeling.

activities.name: Trails, trolls, and Tralmon
activities.description: Great morning with maybe 15 “Expect Delay”-ers on the flank of Tiger Mountain, and then down to coffee and salmon viewing, after a visit to Jakob Two Trees, the troll. Gotta love Issaquah

activities.name: Saving something for this afternoon with Steve
